'''Corey Hall''' (born 7 August 2002) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan and dual registration at the [[York City Knights]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Hall made his [[Super League]] debut in round 14 of the [[2020 Super League season]], in a fixture for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-catalans/teams/54288|title=Starting Lineups - Leeds vs Catalans|publisher=Sky Sports |date=30 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-6-catalans-dragons-34-youthful-rhinos-impress-spirited-display-against-dragons-2988868|title=Leeds Rhinos 6 Catalans Dragons 34: Youthful Rhinos impress in spirited display against Dragons |publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post |first=Peter |last=Smith |date=30 September 2020}}</ref>

==== York City Knights (loan) ====
On 8 July 2021, it was announced that he had signed for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Jul 2021|accessdate=8 Jul 2021|title=Youngster re-signs for Leeds and is loaned out to York|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/youngster-re-signs-for-leeds-and-is-loaned-out-to-york/}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 20 November 2021, it was announced that he had signed to play for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Nov 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Corey Hall leaves Leeds for Wakefield return|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/corey-hall-leeds-wakefield/}}</ref> Hall made 26 appearances and scored 7 tries for Wakefield in his first season with the club.

Hall made only 4 appearances and scored 2 tries for Trinity before his move to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] during the 2023 season.

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 14 April 2023, Hall made his debut for Hull KR, Heritage No. 1288, in a 26-14 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cn|date=August 2023}} Following an impressive showing in his first appearance in red & white, Hall was named in the Round 9 Super League Team of the Week. On 21 April 2023, Hall scored his first try for the Robins in a 7-12 victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at the [[The Jungle (Wheldon Road)|Mend-A-Hose Jungle]].

==== Featherstone Rovers (DR) ====
In April 2024, Hall featured for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] through their dual registration arrangement with Hull KR.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 April 2024|title=Hull KR’s Ben Reynolds returns to Featherstone Rovers on dual-registration following Super League move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-krs-ben-reynolds-returns-to-featherstone-rovers-on-dual-registration-following-super-league-move/}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 23 April 2024, it was announced that Hall would join the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on loan from Hull KR until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Apr 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Castleford’s extraordinary record with signing Hull KR players after Corey Hall deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castlefords-extraordinary-record-with-signing-hull-kr-players-after-corey-hall-deal}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 9 against [[London Broncos|London]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=26 April 2024 |title=Tigers score seven tries to beat winless Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c89zj2n2qx4o |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 12 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 May 2024 |title=Milnes pulls strings as Castleford see off Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw99x3g2w88o |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity (re-join)===
On 19 Jul 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a 3-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Wakefield Trinity|date=19 Jul 2024|accessdate=29 Sep 2024|title=Corey Hall Returns To Wakefield Trinity|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/corey-hall-returns-to-wakefield-trinity/}}</ref>
